tldr: Headlines across all 13 outlet groups uniformly frame this as a White House insider-trading scandal: a staffer with advance access to Trump's speech text placed prediction-market bets and was suspended or fired. Dollar amounts ($90k–$100k+) serve as the primary scandal-scale anchor, while Spanish and Portuguese outlets and Reuters foreground the regulatory-investigation dimension (CFTC probe, insider trading, información privilegiada).

evidence.quote: investigated over alleged trades on Trump speeches
accused of making $100k from Trump speech bets
apostaba sobre sus discursos y ganaba; ya fue destituido
acusado de apostar ilegalmente en Kalshi
Suspenden a funcionario de la Casa Blanca por hacer apuestas
investigado por suspeita de usar informação privilegiada
suspendido por apostar con información anticipada
inside knowledge to win more than $100,000 betting
placed on unpaid leave for betting on his speeches
under CFTC probe over potential insider trading
allegedly made US$90,000 betting on his speeches
suspended over betting allegations: White House

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only headline to lead with an identity hook — 'Who is Gabriel Perez?' — framing the story as a profile of the individual rather than the act or the regulatory consequence.
Only headline to name the specific regulator (CFTC) and label the alleged act as 'insider trading', shifting emphasis from the betting spectacle to the securities-law violation.

bottom_line: At the headline layer, the teleprompter story is a uniform scandal-and-punishment narrative — a White House insider exploited speech access for prediction-market gain and was suspended — with the dollar amounts ($90k–$100k) as the universal hook and only Reuters adding regulatory specificity via the CFTC/insider-trading frame.
